_comment: Spec-derived manifest (cliwright GOAL.md §0/§11). DERIVE it from an ENUMERATED method list — an OpenAPI/Postman/llms.txt, else the docs' full method index or a community machine spec — NOT model recall. Two gates enforce it: scripts/spec-check.sh proves the CLI surface ⊆ this manifest (consistency); scripts/spec-completeness.sh proves this manifest covers ≥90% of api_method_total (completeness). Resources/order are derived from the spec, not chosen by taste.
_completeness_comment: api_method_total = the COUNT of methods/endpoints enumerated from api_method_source (the full API). spec-completeness.sh fails if covered/total < 90% with no recorded waiver in DECISIONS.md. Covered = sum of resources[].verbs + methods[]. A missing/zero total fails the gate (it means enumeration was skipped).
_profile_comment: profile_flag/profile_noun name the multi-profile selector so it reads naturally per API: --bot for Telegram (a profile IS a bot), --instance for n8n, --account for accounting. Default 'profile'. --profile stays as a HIDDEN alias for back-compat. Default 'profile' when nothing fits better.
_methods_comment: RPC-style / non-OpenAPI APIs (e.g. Telegram's sendMessage/getUpdates) that don't map cleanly to resource+verb CRUD MAY instead list flat method names here; spec-completeness.sh counts these toward coverage. Prefer resources[] when the API is REST-shaped.
